data.title: Jasper Therapeutics prices $30M equity offering
data.description:
• Jasper Therapeutics (NASDAQ:JSPR) priced an underwritten public offering of about 11.7 million shares and accompanying warrants at $2.43 each, plus 675,000 pre-funded warrants at the same price minus the $0.0001 per share exercise fee.
  • The offering is expected to raise roughly $30 million before expenses, with proceeds going toward advancing briquilimab, its targeted aglycosylated monoclonal antibody for mast cell-driven diseases, and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on September 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ford recalls ~102K over door trim detachment issue
data.description:
Ford Motor (NYSE:F) is recalling 101,944 vehicles in the U.S. due to a defect that could cause door trim to detach while driving, according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA).

The recall covers certain 2016-2019 Taurus vehicles. The driver and front passenger B-Pillar door trim may detach while driving, creating a road hazard and increasing the risk of a crash, the recall report read.

Dealers will inspect the B-pillar trim, and repair or replace it, as necessary, free of charge.

This latest recall adds to what has already been a record year for Ford in terms of safety recalls. By June 2025, Ford had issued 88 safety recalls, more than any other automaker for a full year, according to the Wall Street Journal. A major recall earlier this year involved 850,000 pickups and SUVs tied to potential fuel-pump failures.

Earlier this month, the automaker recalled 1,456,417 vehicles due to a defect in rear-view cameras.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Lennar misses Q3 estimates amid housing market pressures
Xiaomi to remotely fix assisted driving issue in over 115,000 SU7 cars
data.description:
• Chinese consumer tech giant Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) will issue a software update for over 115,000 of its SU7 electric sedans to fix potential safety issues when its assisted driving features are used, a Chinese regulator said in a product recall notice. 
  • It is the second software fix to fall under product recall rules since the model's launch last year.

data.title: Vor Biopharma trading halted on Thursday, news pending
data.description:
• Vor Biopharma (NASDAQ:VOR) trading was halted on September 18, 2025, pending any announcements from the company. The stock fell over 16% this week.
  • Separately, 10% owner Reprogrammed Interchange and Reid Hoffman, a person who may be deemed a beneficial owner of securities held by Reprogrammed Interchange of VOR (NASDAQ:VOR) sold shares of common stock on September 15.
  • Additionally, the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based company has implemented a 1-for-20 reverse stock split, which was approved by stockholders at a special meeting.
  • The company also announced several board changes in late August 2025.

data.title: The9 buys 51% of gaming JV for $16.2M
data.description:
The9 (NASDAQ:NCTY) on Friday said that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Shanghai The9 Information Technology Co., has entered into a definitive agreement with Shanghai ZhongShun HengXin Network Technology to acquire 51% equity of Shanghai ZhongXinShun Network Technology.

ZhongShun will contribute the exclusive rights of its two proprietary mobile games to the joint venture.

The consideration of the joint venture's 51% equity includes RMB69 million (approximately $9.7 million) and The9 restricted shares with a value of RMB46 million (approximately $6.5 million).

Both payment of cash and restricted shares are based on installments and are only payable according to the actual achievement of the joint venture's profits as committed by ZhongShun in the coming 3 years.

data.title: Deutsche Bank CEO Christian Sewing to face multimillion-pound lawsuit - report
UK's retail sales rise 0.7% Y/Y in August
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK increased 0.70% in August of 2025 over the same month in the previous year, more than estimates of 0.6%.
  • M/M retail sales increased 0.50% in August of 2025.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Coca-Cola bottler to reportedly cut 600+ jobs in South Africa
data.description:
• A bottler for Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) plans to cut more than 600 jobs in South Africa, Business Day reported, citing Food and Allied Workers Union spokesperson Dominique Swartz.
  • Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa has sent workers a notice of possible retrenchment and started a consultation process, the newspaper reported. A final decision hasn’t been made, it said.
  • CCBSA has reportedly cited financial pressures as behind its decision to also shut down plants in Bloemfontein and East London as part of a broader restructuring effort.

data.title: SoftBank Vision Fund to lay off 20% of workforce in shift to bold AI bets - report
data.description:
• SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) to lay off nearly 20% of its Vision Fund team globally as it moves resources to founder Masayoshi Son’s large-scale artificial intelligence bets in the United States, according to a memo seen by Reuters and a source familiar with the plan.
  • The cuts mark the third round of layoffs at the Japanese investment conglomerate’s flagship fund since 2022.
  • While the fund will continue to make new bets, remaining staff will dedicate more resources to Son’s ambitious AI initiatives, such as the proposed $500 billion Stargate project - an initiative to build a vast network of U.S. data centers in partnership with OpenAI (MSFT), the source added.
  • The development comes as SoftBank's (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) joint venture with OpenAI in Japan, which aims to bring AI services to corporate customers, is running significantly behind schedule, according to a separate Reuters report.
